name: Abbey Lincoln
abstract: Anna Marie Wooldridge  (August 6, 1930 – August 14, 2010), known professionally as Abbey Lincoln, was an American jazz vocalist, songwriter, and actress. She was a civil rights activist beginning in the 1960s.

interviews.title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Salutes the Jazz Masters: Verve Records at 50

quotes.text: It'll do. Carnegie Hall. Any place like that. I've been to some beautiful halls in Europe. Yeah. They're sacred halls. The music is sacred, but to a great extent, we're seen as the music of the devil. Black churches and things see us like that. We're not good enough for their sanctified hallowed halls. Yeah.
quotes.caption: Abbey Lincoln reflects on sacred halls and jazz
quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Abbey Lincoln I played for a long time ago. I remember we did a trio thing with Abbey. We played at a club out in Jersey — Teddy Powell’s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: Abbey Lincoln and the Cedar Walton Trio. Must have been a thirteen week engagement at a place named Wells in Harlem.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: The guy said, we’re going to record Abbey Lincoln with Japanese cats and Al Foster. That’s how that happened.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I remember one time we were — I was working with Abbey Lincoln at the Village Vanguard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: And I think that in the industry, and it’s not just in jazz music but it’s all the way across the board, everybody is looking for that person that they can make a star overnight. about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: We did one thing that Thad and Abbey Lincoln had done together. It was called, “Summary.” ... That was one of the tunes I did on there. He also recorded that with Abbey Lincoln. That's right. I think that was just the two of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
